Narrative: On Saturday, January 4th, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report of a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I met the registered owner, who reported that his vehicle was parked in the driveway at 21:30 hours on 01/03/2025. He further reported that he left his residence with his friends in his friend's vehicle and returned to his residence on the morning of 01/04/2025. He noticed his vehicle was taken from his driveway. The registered owner reported there was nothing valuable in the car and was uncertain if the spare keys were in the vehicle. Both New Jersey plates are affixed to the vehicle.      Communications advised me that the vehicle was located in Clarkstown, NY, and was involved in a motor vehicle hit-and-run accident. The registered owner was also advised of the vehicle's location. I observed no cameras in the area.      The victim did not want the vehicle processed or to sign complaints if the suspect(s) are identified.     A New Jersey Victim Notification form was completed and provided to the registered owner.     An ATTF entry was completed    A motor vehicle checklist was completed and submitted.

Narrative: On Tuesday, January 14th, 2025, P.O. Conlon. P.O. Voorhis, and I responded to 450 Hackensack Avenue for a report of a trespasser on the premises who had shoplifted in the past and has also been aggressive with staff in the past.    Upon my arrival, I observed the arrestee enter a white Volkswagon (NJ-Y82VDS) that was leaving the parking lot. The security officers at Home Depot also pointed out the arrestee as he entered the car. I conducted a motor vehicle stop with the vehicle at the traffic light exiting the parking lot. As I approached the vehicle, the arrestee exited the vehicle and started running West on Hackensack Avenue. P.O. Conlon, P.O. Voorhis, and I began running after the arrestee and announcing ourselves as Hackensack Police. When the arrestee approached the other side of Hackensack Avenue, he stopped, and P.O. Conlon apprehended him. The arrestee was flailing his arms while I was attempting to place him in handcuffs. The arrestee was then put in handcuffs with his hands behind his back. The arrestee was then searched, which yielded negative for contraband.   The Loss Prevention Officers stated that the arrestee entered the front door of Home Depot with a black ski covering his face. The loss prevention officers noticed the arrestee from previous shoplifting. The arrestee then concealed several power tools totaling $1,284.00 and placed them in a shopping cart. The arrestee then went past all points of sale and tried to exit the store. Prior to exiting the store, the loss prevention officers approached him and tried to stop him from leaving. The arrestee then pushed the loss prevention officers and fled the store on foot. The loss prevention officer recovered the items. The arrestee then entered the listed vehicle and fled the parking lot for the exit of the mall. A photo copy of the receipt was placed into the BCI mailbox located at the front at the front desk.  The items that were recovered: M18 Jobsite Radio priced at $189.00Two 20V Atomic BL Combo Kit priced at $149.00 eachM18 Fuel Jig Saw priced at $199.00MKE M12 Packout Flood Light priced at $149.00M18 Fuel 10 Miter Saw priced at $449.00     Totaling at $1,284.00.The arrestee was placed in radio car 114 and transported to police headquarters for processing. The vehicle was checked before and after the transport for contraband which yielded negative   The arrestee was processed at police headquarters by P.O. Caero. Complaint W-2025-000016 was authored by Lt. Swetits against the arrestee and was signed for 2C:29-2a(1), 2C:29-2a(2), and two counts of 2C:15-1A(1).  The arrestee also had multiple outstanding warrants from various jurisdictions.The arrestee was remanded to the Bergen County Jail.

Narrative: Suspect 1: Hispanic male, approximately early 30' s in age, approximately 5'10" in height, approximately 180 in weight, wearing a tan hooded sweater, brown "NY" Yankees logo hat, black sweatpants and white sneakers.Suspect 2: Hispanic female, approximately mid 20's in age, approximately 5'5" in height, approximately 140 in weight, wearing a black jacket, grey hooded sweater, tan pants and cream colored shoes. Suspect Vehicle: 4 door Silver in color Hyundai Sonata (unknown registration).On 1/17/25 P.O. Papanikolaou and I responded to 630 Main Street, Target on a reported shoplifting. While   speaking with Target Loss Prevention they wanted to report an additional incident, which occurred in the past.Target Loss Prevention / victim, Cheikh Gaye, reported on 1/16/25 at approximately 21:03 hours suspect 1 and suspect 2 entered Target. It was further reported on 1/16/25 at approximately 21:16 hours suspect 1 and 2 passed all points of sale with unpaid merchandise in a shopping cart. The unpaid merchandise (furniture, grocery, health and beauty, home and kitchen products) totaled $452.71. Prior to exiting Target Loss Prevention / the victim confronted both suspects. Target Loss Prevention were able to recover all merchandise that both suspects attempted to steal. While recovering the stolen merchandise suspect 1 punched the victim in the head, specifically the top left side, with a closed fist one time. The victim reported having a complaint of pain hours after being assaulted. Both suspect later left the area in the suspect vehicle. A Sgt. was requested and Sgt. Competiello responded. Detectives were notified. The victim later responded to headquarters to provide a voluntary statement. The victim refused medical attention at this time.A receipt of the unpaid merchandise was provided by Target Loss Prevention. The receipt was later secured in the Detective Bureau Mailbox.

Narrative: On January 22, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report on a stolen motor vehicle. Upon arrival, I met with the registered owner, Kamil Garcia. The registered owner stated on January 21, 2025 at approximately 1800 hours he parked his vehicle, a 2023 Honda Passport color red bearing NJ registration U22RSV in front of 33 Ross Ave facing West. The registered owner stated that he currently has possession of the two key fobs that belong to the vehicle. At approximately 0309 hours on January 22, 2025 he responded to his vehicle where he last parked it and the vehicle was no longer there.       The registered owner received a completed victim notification. The vehicle was entered as stolen into NCIC (NIC #  V956704006) along with a stolen motor vehicle form. The vehicle was also entered as stolen into the  ATTF website. The registered owner does not wish to have the vehicle processed at this time. Approximate value for the above vehicle is 46,000.00 USD.

Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a reported theft from a motor vehicle. Sgt. Farhi responded to assist. Upon arrival, I spoke with the complainant. She stated that on 01/26/2025 at approximately 20:10 hours, she had turned her vehicle on to heat up. She further stated that she had left the vehicle running with the keys inside when she returned to her apartment for a few moments. The complainant reported that when she returned to the vehicle, the vehicle was still there but the keys were missing.       The complainant stated that no other items appeared to be missing from the vehicle. Furthermore, due to contamination by the complainant, the vehicle was unable to be processed.    The complainant stated that the value of the key fob is approximately $200.00.    The vehicle was parked on Conklin Place, 150 feet east of Pangborn Place, facing west.    A check of the area for cameras revealed one on the Bergen Christian Academies.       A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and provided to the complainant. She was further advised on how to obtain a copy of this report.  Detective Garrett was notified.Vehicle Insurance: GeicoCode :365Policy Number: 6164-87-88-42

Narrative: On Monday, February 3, 2025 at approximately 1640 hours, Sgt. Farhi and I responded to 400 Hackensack Avenue (Marc Jacobs) on a reported robbery. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported that three suspects (1 male, 2 females) entered the establishment at approximately 1600 hours. The suspects reportedly exited and reentered the store multiples time prior to committing the offense, with the last entry being made only by the male suspect.     The victim further reported that the suspect grabbed three small purses from the display shelf before he began to walk toward the exit. At that time, the victim stated that he attempted to stop the suspect, who immediately shoved him into a store fixture. The suspect reportedly proceeded to run out of the store toward the nearest mall exit, where he entered a black vehicle and fled in an unknown direction. The victim stated that he was able to recover one of the purses during the incident.     The victim advised that he sustained minor injuries to his left arm as result of the incident, in which he refused any medical attention. The victim wished to provide a recorded statement to detectives. He was transported to police headquarters where he was turned over to Det. Dogali. The total loss suffered by the establishment for the two purses was approximately $650.00. The store manager (witness) wished to have complaints signed against the suspects if they were able to be identified.     Suspect 1: Black male, slim build, 5'8", black beanie, black puffer, dark pants, white/blue sneakers     Suspect 2: Black female, heavy build, 5'8", short hair, black puffer, black leggings, black pocketbook     Suspect 3: Black female, heavy build, 5'7", long cornrows, gray puffer, light blue jeans

Narrative: On Tuesday, 01/14/2025, P.O. Rivera and I responded to 895 Main Street apartment 12A for a Domestic Violence incident report. Mrs. Charmaine Perez (victim) is the mother of Mr. Christopher Pereira (suspect). Mr. Eusebia Perez (witness) is married to the victim and stepfather to the suspect.      The victim reported that the suspect has not lived at the apartment for approximately three (3) months. The suspect does not have key access to the apartment. There is a vestibule, which is kept locked, that leads to a first and second floor apartment. The victim's apartment is on the first floor.       The victim reported that the suspect broke into the apartment uninvited and continued into her bedroom, where she was located. The suspect was irate, confronted her about money that she was awarded from a lawsuit settlement. The suspect stated, " BITCH, DON'T MESS WITH THE GRANDKIDS, DO YOU KNOW WHO THAT NIGGA IS ON THE COUCH, STAY THE FUCK AWAY FROM ME!" The victim reported at the time her granddaughter, Ms. Keka Ornsby, and her boyfriend were seated on the couch when the suspect entered. The victim reported that the suspect did not engage with Ms. Keka Ornsby. It should be noted, Ms. Ornsby's mother has an active temporary restraining order (Docket #FV-02000101-11 against the suspect. Ms. Ornsby is listed as an additional person in the order. The victim reported that Ms. Ornsby began staying with her for approximately three (3) weeks. Due to the statements the victim made, we did not sign a complaint against suspect for 2C:29-9 (Contempt of court).       The victim reported when the suspect exited the bedroom, the granddaughter and her boyfriend left and the witness entered the apartment. The witness reported that he avoided confrontation from the suspect by exiting the apartment. The witness was unaware the suspect was inside and the victim confirmed his reporting. It should be noted, the suspect has a no contact order against the witness (See complaint #W2024-000767-0223). Due to the victim's and witness's statement, we did not sign a complaint against the witness for 2C:29-9 (Contempt of court).      Sgt. Williams arrived to assist. The victim believed the suspect used a credit card to gain entry into the apartment. The vestibule door and apartment door had old damage near the doorknob and strike plate. The victim confirmed our observation. Sgt. Detective Bay was notified. The scene was unable to be processed, but was captured by my body worn camera.      The victim wanted a temporary restraining order and willing to give a recorded statement. She was transported to headquarters, gave a recorded statement to detectives (See their supplement), and Judge Takvorian granted the TRO in report room #2 @1342 hours. Sgt. Feuilly was sent an e-mail to preserve the recording. The victim was given her copy of the TRO. She did not want to speak with a Domestic Violence Response member, but was provided their phone number. A voluntary handwritten form was not completed because she gave a recorded statement. A New Jersey Victim notification form was completed.      Lt. Knapp authored complaint #W-0223-2025-000014 for 2C:33-4C (Harassment), a petty disorderly persons offense, and 2C:18-2A(1) (Burglary), a third degree crime.

Narrative: Victim and suspect were in a dating relationship for approximately 9 months.    I responded to Police Headquarters on a report of Domestic Violence. Upon arrival I met with the Victim who stated on 02/13/2025 at approximately 0200 hours she was at a bar called "Six26" in Jersey City, NJ (128 Christopher Columbus Drive, Jersey City, NJ 07302.) She stated while she was leaving the bar, the suspect met with her on the sidewalk. The victim stated the suspect wished to get back together, but she refused. He then became angry and proceeded to strangle her, briefly cutting off her ability to breath, however she did not lose consciousness. Right after the suspect strangled her, he stole her Iphone 14 Pro out of her front pocket (Approximate value: $1,000.00) then fled the scene.     The victim advised me that she currently has a restraining order against the suspect, but he has not been served. Docket number: FV-BER-002857-24.A recorded statement was taken by Detective Mora. BREATHE services were offered but the victim refused. A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed.DVRT Form was completed.Voluntary Handwritten Statement was completed.ODARA Form was completed. Jersey City Police Department Detective Soto was advised of the incident.All paperwork was forwarded to him for complaints to be signed.

Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown     I responded to the listed location, 35 Pangborn Place - Hackensack Emergency Dental, on a report of a burglary. Upon arrival, I was met by the complainant, Beau Jin, who is the manager of the business. The complainant stated that a TP-Link 300 Mbps Wireless Router ($100.00 USD) and a Keep Connect Router Reporter ($50.00) had been a taken from the basement. The complainant further stated there did not appear to be any forced entry into the basement. I was then advised by the complainant that the basement was accessible by the employees as well as the residence of the apartments on the second and third floor.     The complainant advised me the business was closed and secured for on 02/14/2025 until 02/16/2025. The complainant further advised me he on 02/15/2025 he observed through his phone that the businesses router was inoperable. The complainant stated upon opening business on 02/17/2025 he responded to the basement and observed that the router was taken. The complainant further reported he observed feces on the handle of the main entrance door and in the amazon drop off box located in front of the business.      The scene was unable to be processed due to the complainant contamination the scene as well as the fecal matter contaminating the door handle.Sgt. Farhi was notified. Detectives were notified by Sgt. Farhi. Total value of items taken - $150.00 USD
